senses.definition: Acts or processes of putting trains or wheels back onto the rails after they have come off.
examples: The maintenance report listed three rerailings after minor derailments during the storm.
Emergency crews carried out several rerailings before traffic could resume on the line.

senses.definition: Puts a train, wheel, or similar object back onto the rails after it has come off.
examples: The team quickly rerails the carriage to minimize delays.
A special device rerails derailed freight cars automatically.

senses.definition: In card games, especially poker, to raise the bet again after another player has already raised.
examples: He decides to reraise to put more pressure on his opponents.
If you reraise here, you might force the weaker hands to fold.

senses.definition: To raise or bring up a topic, issue, or amount again.
examples: The report will reraise questions about safety at the plant.
They decided not to reraise the budget issue at the meeting.

senses.definition: Raised a bet again after another player had already raised, especially in poker.
examples: She reraised on the turn to maximize value from her strong hand.
He reraised and everyone else folded immediately.

senses.definition: Raised or brought up a topic, issue, or amount again.
examples: The article reraised old concerns about data privacy.
The committee reraised the question of funding at the end.

senses.definition: Raises a bet again after another player has already raised, especially in poker.
examples: When he reraises, most players at the table fold.
She usually reraises with premium hands only.

senses.definition: Raises or brings up a topic, issue, or amount again.
examples: The new study reraises the problem of long-term funding.
This incident reraises questions about staff training.

senses.definition: The action of raising a bet again after it has already been raised, especially in poker.
examples: Reraising can be a powerful strategy against aggressive players.
Constant reraising made the pot very large by the river.

senses.definition: Raising a bet again after another player has already raised, especially in poker.
examples: She is reraising to isolate the weaker player.
By reraising here, you show a lot of strength.

senses.definition: To rake something again, for example soil, leaves, or a surface.
examples: We had to rerake the gravel path after the heavy rain.
The gardener rerakes the lawn to remove the last of the leaves.

senses.definition: Showed, did, or performed something again, especially a program, process, or test.
examples: They reran the experiment to confirm the results.
The network reran the documentary due to popular demand.

senses.definition: To change the order of things in a ranking or list, usually based on new information.
examples: The system will rerank the results after you apply these filters.
Universities may rerank applicants when new exam scores arrive.

senses.definition: To give something a new rate, value, or price, especially in finance or insurance.
examples: The company decided to rerate its insurance policies after the floods.
Analysts may rerate the stock if profits continue to fall.

senses.definition: Given a new rate, value, or price, especially in finance or insurance.
examples: Several bonds were rerated following the economic slowdown.
Our policy was rerated after we reported the renovations.

senses.definition: The act of giving something a new rate, value, or price, especially in finance or insurance.
examples: A rerating of the company’s debt is expected next quarter.
Policy rerating led to higher premiums for many customers.

senses.definition: To read something again.
examples: I often reread my notes before an exam.
She reread the email to make sure she understood it correctly.

senses.definition: A person who reads texts, especially books, more than once.
examples: As a dedicated rereader, he returns to the same novels every year.
The book club is full of rereaders who enjoy revisiting classics.

senses.definition: The act of reading something again.
examples: Rereading can help you notice details you missed the first time.
A quick rereading of the instructions solved the problem.

senses.definition: Occasions or acts of reading something again.
examples: After several rereadings, the poem began to make more sense.
Her repeated rereadings of the contract revealed a small error.

senses.definition: Reads something again.
examples: He always rereads his messages before sending them.
She rereads the article every time a new update appears.

senses.definition: A piece of plate armour that protects the upper part of the arm between the shoulder and the elbow.
examples: The knight’s rerebrace was decorated with his family crest.
Museum visitors examined the dented rerebrace from the battlefield.

senses.definition: Pieces of plate armour that protect the upper parts of the arms between the shoulders and the elbows.
examples: The suit of armour includes rerebraces, gauntlets, and greaves.
Both rerebraces show signs of heavy use in combat.

senses.definition: To record something again, especially music or audio.
examples: The band decided to rerecord their first single with better equipment.
They had to rerecord the dialogue because of background noise.

senses.definition: Recorded something again, especially music or audio.
examples: The song was rerecorded for the anniversary edition of the album.
They rerecorded the speech in a quieter studio.

senses.definition: The act of recording something again, especially music or audio.
examples: Rerecording the soundtrack took several weeks.
The film went through extensive dialogue rerecording in post-production.

senses.definition: Records something again, especially music or audio.
examples: The artist often rerecords old hits for special releases.
The team rerecords the lines whenever they hear a mistake.

senses.definition: A latrine or toilet block at the back of a medieval monastery or convent.
examples: The ruins of the reredorter can still be seen behind the cloister.
Archaeologists uncovered the drainage system of the old reredorter.

senses.definition: Latrines or toilet blocks at the backs of medieval monasteries or convents.
examples: The plan shows separate reredorters for monks and lay brothers.
Few medieval reredorters have survived in good condition.

senses.definition: A decorated wood or stone screen or wall behind the altar in a church.
examples: The church’s stone reredos is covered with carved figures of saints.
Restorers cleaned the painted reredos behind the main altar.

senses.definition: Decorated wood or stone screens or walls behind altars in churches.
examples: The cathedral contains several richly decorated reredoses.
Medieval reredoses were often brightly painted.

senses.definition: An older or less common spelling of reredos, meaning a decorated screen or wall behind an altar in a church.
examples: The inventory lists a ‘reredosse’ of carved oak behind the altar.
Scholars debate the original colours of the great reredosse.

senses.definition: Older or less common spellings of reredoses, meaning decorated screens or walls behind church altars.
examples: The abbey once had several painted reredosses.
Inventories mention ‘reredosses’ of wood and stone.

senses.definition: An archaic form of rear, referring to the back part of something.
examples: The sailors gathered on the reree deck as the ship left port.
A small reree cabin was added to the back of the house.

senses.definition: To wind thread, film, or tape onto another reel again.
examples: They had to rereel the old film before digitizing it.
The worker carefully rereels the yarn to remove any twists.

senses.definition: To pass a rope through a block, ring, or hole again, especially on a ship.
examples: The sailors had to rereeve the line through the pulley.
They rereeve the rope to adjust the rigging.

senses.definition: In feudal law, a fief or piece of land held from another fief, rather than directly from the king.
examples: The knight’s small rerefief was held through a powerful earl.
Disputes over a rerefief could involve several different lords.

senses.definition: To register something or someone again, by putting details back onto an official list.
examples: You must reregister your car if you move to another state.
Users have to reregister their accounts after a long period of inactivity.

senses.definition: Registered something or someone again, by putting details back onto an official list.
examples: Her parents reregistered the car in her name when she turned 21.
The company reregistered its trademark after the merger.

senses.definition: Registering something or someone again, by putting details back onto an official list.
examples: Reregistering your vehicle is necessary after you change address.
They are reregistering students who missed last semester.

senses.definition: Registers something or someone again, by putting details back onto an official list.
examples: The owner reregisters the car every time he moves to a new state.
If no activity is detected, the system reregisters users after one year.

senses.definition: The act or process of registering something or someone again.
examples: Voter reregistration is required after a long absence from the country.
The reregistration of vehicles must be completed within thirty days of moving.

senses.definition: Acts or instances of registering something or someone again.
examples: Online reregistrations increased after the new system was introduced.
The office processed hundreds of vehicle reregistrations in one week.

senses.definition: To bring something under new or renewed government or official control by making new rules for it.
examples: The government plans to reregulate the energy market after years of liberalization.
Some argue that we should reregulate banks to prevent another crisis.

senses.definition: Brought something under new or renewed government or official control with new rules.
examples: After the scandal, the sector was heavily reregulated.
Several industries were reregulated during that period.

senses.definition: Brings something under new or renewed government or official control with new rules.
examples: The proposed law reregulates how data can be shared between companies.
The agency reregulates the industry when risks become too high.

senses.definition: Bringing something under new or renewed government or official control with new rules.
examples: Reregulating financial markets is a major political issue.
The government is reregulating the sector after a series of failures.

senses.definition: The act of bringing an industry or activity back under government or official control with new rules.
examples: Many voters support the reregulation of public utilities.
Reregulation of the banking sector followed the financial crisis.

senses.definition: Instances or sets of new rules that bring industries or activities back under official control.
examples: Different reregulations were introduced for energy, water, and transport.
The new reregulations aim to protect consumers.

senses.definition: To rule or reign again after having reigned before.
examples: In the legend, the king returns to rereign over a united land.
Some thought the prince might one day rereign after his exile.

senses.definition: To make a book, film, album, or other product available to the public again.
examples: The studio plans to rerelease the classic film in high definition.
They will rerelease the novel with a new introduction.

senses.definition: A new release of a book, film, album, or other product that was available before.
examples: The film’s rerelease attracted a new generation of fans.
Collectors are excited about the limited rerelease on vinyl.

senses.definition: Made a book, film, album, or other product available to the public again.
examples: The album was rereleased with bonus tracks.
The studio rereleased the movie to celebrate its 20th anniversary.

senses.definition: Makes a book, film, album, or other product available to the public again.
examples: Each year, the company rereleases several classic games.
The label rereleases the record with improved sound quality.

senses.definition: New releases of books, films, albums, or other products that were available before.
examples: Recent rereleases have introduced the band to younger listeners.
Studios schedule their big rereleases for holiday periods.

senses.definition: Making a book, film, album, or other product available to the public again.
examples: They are rereleasing the classic trilogy with restored footage.
Rereleasing old albums can open up new income streams.
